prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly black frame. A small white watermark reading “Nostalgic X’er” appears at the lower right.

After about twelve seconds, the image cuts to a close-up of camera equipment laid out on a surface. A Polaroid-style instant camera and/or related device is visible, along with a boxy accessory or film pack. Hands are partially visible near the items, suggesting someone is handling or presenting the equipment. The image quality is soft and somewhat blurred, so small printed details on the objects are not readable.

The next shot shows a living-room setting. An adult man with gray hair sits on a sofa or chair holding a boxy instant camera facing the viewer. A young boy sits close beside him, watching. The background includes domestic décor such as flowers, curtains, furniture, and a lamp, giving the scene a home interior atmosphere. The man appears to be demonstrating or showing the camera.

Later, the man turns toward the boy while holding a small rectangular instant photograph or card in one hand and a cup or mug in the other. The camera rests against him or hangs near his chest. The boy looks at the photograph/card. Their body language suggests the man is showing the boy the result of using the camera.

In the final frame, large white title text appears over the living-room shot. The visible text reads: “Polaroid’s Pronto! Flash 400 Land Cameras.” The man and boy remain seated behind the text, facing each other. The “Nostalgic X’er” watermark remains at the lower right throughout the visible frames.
